data.narrative:
Telexistence, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jin Tomioka, Ken Tomoyose, and Susumu Tachi, has raised $170M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of nine comparable companies: one public entity (277810.KQ) and eight private firms including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat, all at the same stage.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the $170M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a $323M valuation. Peer-median methodology captures six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.1B and a range spanning $300M to $10.0B, driven substantially by 1X Technologies' $10.0B outlier valuation against more clustered valuations of $1.8B, $1.6B, $500M, and $350M among the other four peers.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $275M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $699M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in the peer set—particularly whether Telexistence's technology roadmap and go-to-market trajectory more closely resemble the outlier ($10B) or the tighter cluster ($350M–$1.8B). Capital deployment efficiency relative to peer benchmarks and near-term milestone probability are key drivers of positioning within this range.
